2604.13899.title: Do We Still Need Humans in the Loop? Comparing Human and LLM Annotation in Active Learning for Hostility Detection
2604.13899.paper_summary: Instruction-tuned LLMs can annotate thousands of instances from a short prompt at negligible cost. This raises two questions for active learning (AL): can LLM labels replace human labels within the AL loop, and does AL remain necessary when entire corpora can be labelled at once? We investigate both questions on a new dataset of 277,902 German political TikTok comments (25,974 LLM-labelled, 5,000 human-annotated), comparing seven annotation strategies across four encoders to detect anti-immigrant hostility. A classifier trained on 25,974 GPT-5.2 labels (\$43) achieves comparable F1-Macro to one trained on 3,800 human annotations (\$316). Active learning offers little advantage over random sampling in our pre-enriched pool and delivers lower F1 than full LLM annotation at the same cost. However, comparable aggregate F1 masks a systematic difference in error structure: LLM-trained classifiers over-predict the positive class relative to the human gold standard. This divergence concentrates in topically ambiguous discussions where the distinction between anti-immigrant hostility and policy critique is most subtle, suggesting that annotation strategy should be guided not by aggregate F1 alone but by the error profile acceptable for the target application.
2604.13899.paper_summary_zh: 指令調整的 LLM 可以以微不足道的成本從短提示中標註數千個實例。這對於主動學習（AL）提出了兩個問題：LLM 標籤能否在 AL 循環中取代人類標籤，以及當整個語料庫可以一次性標註時，AL 是否仍然必要？我們在一個新的數據集上調查這兩個問題，該數據集包含 277,902 條德國政治 TikTok 評論（25,974 條 LLM 標註，5,000 條人類標註），比較七種標註策略在四個編碼器上的表現，以檢測反移民敵意。基於 25,974 條 GPT-5.2 標籤（\$43）訓練的分類器達到了與基於 3,800 條人類標註（\$316）訓練的分類器相當的 F1-Macro。主動學習在我們的預先豐富池中對隨機抽樣幾乎沒有優勢，並且在相同成本下提供的 F1 低於完整的 LLM 標註。然而，可比的總體 F1 掩蓋了錯誤結構的系統性差異：相對於人類金標準，LLM 訓練的分類器對正類的預測過高。這種差異集中在主題模糊的討論中，在這些討論中，反移民敵意和政策批評之間的區別最為微妙，這表明標註策略應該不僅僅由總體 F1 指導，而應由目標應用可接受的錯誤輪廓來指導。
2604.13899.author: Ahmad Dawar Hakimi et.al.
2604.13899.authors: Ahmad Dawar Hakimi, Lea Hirlimann, Isabelle Augenstein, Hinrich Schütze
